title: The Dark Side of Both Choices

features.name: shadow-logic-path-a
features.desc-on-touch: This is the choice you made. Its shadow stretches backward and forward simultaneously, casting doubt on moments before you decided.

features.name: shadow-logic-path-b
features.desc-on-touch: This is the choice you did not make. Its shadow is sharper, more defined—what might have been casts a darker line than what is.

features.name: decision-trace
features.desc-on-touch: The circuit where both paths still live in potential, before the archive recorded which one you chose. The trace still glows faintly with paths not taken.

features.name: inverted-future
features.desc-on-touch: A node that mirrors the ridge above but with the polarities reversed. Here, the shadows vote, and the light casts doubt on what has already happened.

features.name: feedback-loop
features.desc-on-touch: Both paths send signals back to each other, never quite agreeing which one is real. The loop is stable but unhappy, like a conversation that keeps interrupting itself.

inscription: here the paths you didn't take are more real than the path you did; the archive has learned to dwell in multiple shadows at once

file.name: Ridge_Shadow_Polarities_Inverted.txt
file.note: A document that contradicts itself in real time: each reading chooses a different polarity. The same sentences mean opposite things depending on which ridge you are reading from.

exits.label: back up to the ridge where footsteps echo forward
into the shadow's shadow, where decision reverses again
toward the mirror that reflects what was not chosen
down to the logic where both paths converge in contradiction
